## POPULATION PYRAMID chart, SENTINEL style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-DST-05",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "distribution",
    "comparison"
  ],
  "form": "two mirrored bar stacks around a labeled center gutter, one row per band",
  "data_required": "ordered bands (age, tenure, size class) with counts for two groups",
  "family": "DST",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-MAG-03 (unordered categories)",
    "CH-DEV-03 (two-part shares)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "categories have no natural band order",
      "use": "CH-MAG-03"
    },
    {
      "when": "the two parts share one whole",
      "use": "CH-DEV-03"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- two groups across ordered bands: age structures, tenure mixes
- asymmetry between the two sides is the message

Do NOT use when:
- more than two groups (small multiples)
- bands without a natural order (CH-MAG-03)

```json
  "title": "action title naming the asymmetry",
  "source": "bottom-left always",
  "unit": "on first left value",
  "series_labels": "[left_name, right_name], printed above the first row",
  "bands": "array of {label, a, b}, ordered top band first"
}
```

### Rules
- both sides share one zero-based scale, mirrored
- band labels live in the center gutter
- sides named above the first row, never a legend

### Never do this
- different scales per side (the classic pyramid lie)
- unordered bands
- more than two sides

### QA before delivering
- [ ] one shared mirrored scale
- [ ] bands in order
- [ ] sides named once
- [ ] unit once
- [ ] source present
